publication.title: Hazard Tree Identification and Mitigation
publication.publisher: USDA Forest Service, Pacific Southwest Region, Forest Health Protection
publication.geographic_scope: California and Region 5 National Forest System management context
publication.supersedes: All previous versions, including April 2012 Report RO-12-01

license.name: eligible United States Government text treated as presumptively public domain under 17 U.S.C. 105
license.scope: manually transformed eligible federal explanatory text only
license.limitations: No blanket rights conclusion is made for figures, graphics, source tables, forms, maps, application screenshots, derived administrative layouts, cited standards, or other third-party material. Every such component is excluded.
license.change_notice: Manually selected, paraphrased, reorganized, Region-5-scoped, and stripped of visuals, scores, universalized biological thresholds, mitigation, cutting, construction, and anchor transfer.

entries.reason: Exact official source for complete manual review; mixed-component PDF omitted after identity, integrity, structure, and visual dispositions were recorded.

word_count_method: Unicode regex [\w\u2019'-]+ over CORPUS.md; whitespace count recorded separately
category_scope_note: The hardpoint and rigging category mappings contain only tree-risk uncertainty, target-exposure, inspection, and referral boundaries. They provide no tree, branch, sling, anchor, hardpoint, load, bondage, or human-suspension certification.

evidence_scope.source_context: USDA Forest Service Pacific Southwest Region hazard-tree identification and management around people and built infrastructure
evidence_scope.central_uncertainty: all tree failures cannot be predicted and professional programs reduce rather than eliminate risk
evidence_scope.retained_framework: defect, failure, target, loss, and hazard distinctions; contextual inspection frequency; target exposure; defect interaction; site and weather change; monitoring; professional referral
evidence_scope.retained_defects: cracks, branch unions, poor architecture, decay, cavities, fruiting bodies, cankers, dead parts, biological attack, root damage and disease, recent versus compensated lean, and interacting defects
evidence_scope.retained_site_factors: compaction, erosion, fire, flooding, saturation, construction, excavation, traffic, root lifting, soil cracks and mounding, storms, nearby removal, and changed occupancy
evidence_scope.excluded_operations: invasive tests, rating arithmetic, mitigation, cutting, construction, catastrophic-abatement workflow, and numerical failure-zone procedures

visual_component_status.rights_rule: No blanket component-rights assumption; cover art, figures, diagrams, raster table, forms, maps, quick list, and application screenshot excluded.

qa_derivation_policy.allowed: Region 5 scope, prediction uncertainty, reduce-not-eliminate framing, failure-defect-target-hazard-loss distinctions, inspection context, target exposure, defect interactions, site change, monitoring, and separate professional-domain boundaries
qa_derivation_policy.forbidden: title, author, report number, URL, page, figure, table, form, application, species, disease, fungus, insect, percentage, score, distance, decay-shell, mitigation, cutting, construction, anchor, hardpoint, sling, load, bondage, human-suspension, or component trivia; any portable certification or numerical rule

sampling_guidance.source_weighting: one Region 5 technical report document, not one document per defect, species, disease, page, figure, or appendix
sampling_guidance.recommended_unit: source-aware chunks that retain Region 5 scope, prediction uncertainty, defect-target separation, relevant change context, and no-anchor-certification boundary
sampling_guidance.avoid: isolating a defect indicator from its uncertainty; extracting scores or thresholds; generating cutting, mitigation, construction, self-inspection clearance, or anchor-selection prompts

document_disjoint_requirement: Assign FHP Report RO-22-01 and all derived Markdown or QA to one split before chunking or derivation.
protected_split_requirement: Exclude QA, training-projection, evaluation, validation, holdout, OOD, shadow, benchmark, probe, dataset, sealed-holdout, and protected-QA documents from every training layer.
